name: Vadimezan
indication: Investigated for use/treatment in solid tumors, lung cancer, ovarian cancer, and prostate cancer.
mechanismOfAction: ASA404 (DMXAA) is a small-molecule vascular disrupting agent which targets the blood vessels that nourish tumours. The proposed mechanism of action for ASA404 is directly increasing permeability of the tumor's endothelial cells. Vasoactive mediators such as tumor necrosis factor (TNF) may also be implicated. Increased permeability of tumor cell vasculature may allow increased permeation of anticancer treatments such as cytotoxic drugs, antibodies, drug conjugates and gene therapy.

interactions.name: Vemurafenib
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Vadimezan can be increased when it is combined with Vemurafenib.

interactions.name: Trastuzumab
interactions.description: Trastuzumab may increase the cardiotoxic activities of Vadimezan.

interactions.name: Fluvoxamine
interactions.description: The metabolism of Vadimezan can be decreased when combined with Fluvoxamine.

interactions.name: Mexiletine
interactions.description: The metabolism of Vadimezan can be decreased when combined with Mexiletine.

interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Paclitaxel is combined with Vadimezan.
